hypothesis: A multimodal contrastive model that jointly aligns SMILES strings with 3D electron density voxel grids will produce embeddings that are invariant to arbitrary 3D rotations of the density (emergent pose-invariance) and consequently yield higher rotation-robust retrieval accuracy, greater multi-pose retrieval rates, and improved structure–property clustering and linear-probe property prediction compared to unimodal 3D encoders and SE(3)-equivariant baselines.
